title: Nachiketa's Journey & Boons
summary: Nachiketa travels to Death's abode and receives three boons, seeking to know what happens to a person after death. Addresses fear of death, mortality, afterlife, and grief over deceased loved ones.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Yearning ( superior rewards,) Vajasravasa gifted all his material wealth. He had a son named Nachiketa.” and develops toward “(Nachiketa said): "Upon the death of a person, there arises this doubt - some declare that something outlives the body, other say, nothing remains. I…”

This passage begins with “(Yama said) : " This subtle matter is not easily understood. In the days of yore, even Gods had entairtained doubts about this. Therefore, O! Nachiketa! choose another boon. Do not press…” and develops toward “O! Death! Instruct me on the superior life after death regarding which people have reservations. Nachiketa chooses not other boon other than this…”

verses.sanskrit: ॐ उशन् ह वै वाजश्रवसः सर्ववेदसं ददौ । तस्य ह नचिकेता नाम पुत्र आस ॥
verses.translation: Yearning ( superior rewards,) Vajasravasa gifted all his material wealth. He had a son named Nachiketa.

verses.sanskrit: तँ ह कुमारँ सन्तं दक्षिणासु नीयमानासु श्रद्धाविवेश सोऽमन्यत ॥
verses.translation: On the gifts being fetched for giving , Nachiketa, though a young lad, was overcome with religious fervour and he pondered :

verses.sanskrit: पीतोदका जग्धतृणा दुग्धदोहा निरिन्द्रियाः । अनन्दा नाम ते लोकास्तान् स गच्छति ता ददत् ॥
verses.translation: Joyless, indeed, are the worlds to which he goes who gives as gifts such old cows that cannot calve nor produce milk any more. They had also drank water for the last time and eaten grass terminally. (Giving away useless articles as gift in a sacrifice is a sin and cannot bring good reward.)

verses.sanskrit: स होवाच पितरं तत कस्मै मां दास्यसीति । द्वितीयं तृतीयं तँ होवाच मृत्यवे त्वा ददामीति ॥
verses.translation: He addressed his father : "Father, to whom will you give me away?" He repeatedly said so, twice and thrice. Upon this the father replied : "I shall give you to Death"

verses.sanskrit: बहूनामेमि प्रथमो बहूनामेमि मध्यमः । किँ स्विद्यमस्य कर्तव्यं यन्मयाऽद्य करिष्यति ॥
verses.translation: (Nachiketa thought within himself) : Among many of (my father's disciples), I am either at the top or middle (but never at the bottom ). What then will he achieve in giving me to Yama ?

verses.sanskrit: अनुपश्य यथा पूर्वे प्रतिपश्य तथाऽपरे । सस्यमिव मर्त्यः पच्यते सस्यमिवाजायते पुनः ॥
verses.translation: Remember how your forefathers behaved . Consider how men of worth conduct themselves today. Like vegetation man ripens and decays, and like vegetation he is born again .

verses.sanskrit: वैश्वानरः प्रविशत्यतिथिर्ब्राह्मणो गृहान् । तस्यैताँ शान्तिं कुर्वन्ति हर वैवस्वतोदकम् ॥
verses.translation: (Nachiketa goes to the abode of Yama, but the latter was not at home. Nachiketa had to wait for three days without food. On Yama's return, he is told by his people): A Brahmana guest entering a house-hold resembles fire. He is offered appeasement. O ! Vaivasvata (Yama), fetch water (for washing the feet and sipping).

verses.sanskrit: आशाप्रतीक्षे संगतँ सूनृतां चेष्टापूर्ते पुत्रपशूँश्च सर्वान् । एतद्वृङ्क्ते पुरुषस्याल्पमेधसो यस्यानश्नन्वसति ब्राह्मणो गृहे ॥
verses.translation: Hopes and expectations, the benefits of association with the good, the merit arising from sweet and beneficial speech, the deeds prescribed in the scriptures and moral codes , children and cattle, all these are destroyed in the case of the person of dull intellect in whose house a holy guest has remained unattended.

verses.sanskrit: तिस्रो रात्रीर्यदवात्सीर्गृहे मे- ऽनश्नन् ब्रह्मन्नतिथिर्नमस्यः । नमस्तेऽस्तु ब्रह्मन् स्वस्ति मेऽस्तु तस्मात्प्रति त्रीन्वरान्वृणीष्व ॥
verses.translation: Yama said to Nachiketa : O holy and venerable guest, since you have dwelt in my house for three nights without food, choose three boons from me. I bow to you; may all be well with me.

verses.sanskrit: शान्तसंकल्पः सुमना यथा स्याद् वीतमन्युर्गौतमो माऽभि मृत्यो । त्वत्प्रसृष्टं माऽभिवदेत्प्रतीत एतत् त्रयाणां प्रथमं वरं वृणे ॥
verses.translation: (Nachiketa thereupon said) - O Death, may I have the first of the three boons to (my father) Gautama being in happy frame of mind and of peaceful disposition anger; and may he recognise me and receive me when I am released by you.

verses.sanskrit: यथा पुरस्ताद् भविता प्रतीत औद्दालकिरारुणिर्मत्प्रसृष्टः । सुखँ रात्रीः शयिता वीतमन्यु स्त्वां ददृशिवान्मृत्युमुखात् प्रमुक्तम् ॥
verses.translation: (Yama said :) By my order, Auddalaki Aruni (your father) will sleep peacefully at night. He will recognise you and treat you as in the past, having seen you released from the jaws of death. And he will be without anger . (The mental agony caused to Nachiketa's father has been removed by the first boon).

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्गे लोके न भयं किंचनास्ति न तत्र त्वं न जरया बिभेति । उभे तीर्त्वाऽशनायापिपासे शोकातिगो मोदते स्वर्गलोके ॥
verses.translation: (Nachiketa said:) In heaven there is no fear at all ; death and old age are not there. Having overcome hunger and thirst and all sorrow, one rejoices in the heavenly world.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वमग्निँ स्वर्ग्यमध्येषि मृत्यो प्रब्रूहि त्वँ श्रद्दधानाय मह्यम् । स्वर्गलोका अमृतत्वं भजन्त एतद् द्वितीयेन वृणे वरेण ॥
verses.translation: O Death, You know the Fire (sacrifice) that leads to heaven. Instruct me on that who am full of faith . Those in heaven are deathless. This I choose as my second boon.

verses.sanskrit: प्र ते ब्रवीमि तदु मे निबोध स्वर्ग्यमग्निं नचिकेतः प्रजानन् । अनन्तलोकाप्तिमथो प्रतिष्ठां विद्धि त्वमेतं निहितं गुहायाम् ॥
verses.translation: O Nachiketa, I know about the Fire that leads to heaven. This I shall impart to you . Listen to me carefully. Know this to be the means of attaining eternal heaven and also the support of the world. This knowledge is hidden in the cave of the heart.

verses.sanskrit: लोकादिमग्निं तमुवाच तस्मै या इष्टका यावतीर्वा यथा वा । स चापि तत्प्रत्यवदद्यथोक्त मथास्य मृत्युः पुनरेवाह तुष्टः ॥
verses.translation: Yama then explained that Fire (sacrifice), is the origin of the world. He gave details of what type of bricks and how many are required (for the altar) and how the fire is to be lit. And Nachiketa repeated all the instructions given to him. Then Yama, being pleased at this, said again.

verses.sanskrit: तमब्रवीत् प्रीयमाणो महात्मा वरं तवेहाद्य ददामि भूयः । तवैव नाम्ना भविताऽयमग्निः सृङ्कां चेमामनेकरूपां गृहाण ॥
verses.translation: That high-souled (Yama) happily, said to Nachiketa "I shall here and now give you another boon: may this fire be named after you; and may you also accept this multi-coloured garland.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिणाचिकेतस्त्रिभिरेत्य सन्धिं त्रिकर्मकृत्तरति जन्ममृत्यू । ब्रह्मजज्ञं देवमीड्यं विदित्वा निचाय्येमाँ शान्तिमत्यन्तमेति ॥
verses.translation: He transcends cyclic existence who has perfomed the Nachiketa fire-sacrifice three times and has received instructions from the three (mother,father and preceptor), and has also discharged his three-fold duties (study of Vedas, performance of Sacrifice and giving alms), having realised the all pervading shining one worthy of worship-the fire god born of Brahman. Such a person also attains supreme peace.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिणाचिकेतस्त्रयमेतद्विदित्वा य एवं विद्वाँश्चिनुते नाचिकेतम् । स मृत्युपाशान् पुरतः प्रणोद्य शोकातिगो मोदते स्वर्गलोके ॥
verses.translation: The man of wisdom, having known the injunctions performs the Nachiketa sacrifice three times. Thus annhilating the noose of death even before the fall of his body, crosses the (ocean of ) grief and enjoys in heaven.

verses.sanskrit: एष तेऽग्निर्नचिकेतः स्वर्ग्यो यमवृणीथा द्वितीयेन वरेण । एतमग्निं तवैव प्रवक्ष्यन्ति जनासः तृतीयं वरं नचिकेतो वृणीष्व ॥
verses.translation: This is your fire, which takes one to heaven and which you have chosen as your second boon. People will designate this fire after your name. Now choose your third boon, O Nachiketa.

verses.sanskrit: येयं प्रेते विचिकित्सा मनुष्ये- ऽस्तीत्येके नायमस्तीति चैके । एतद्विद्यामनुशिष्टस्त्वयाऽहं वराणामेष वरस्तृतीयः ॥
verses.translation: (Nachiketa said): "Upon the death of a person, there arises this doubt - some declare that something outlives the body, other say, nothing remains. I desire to know, upon your instructions, about this issue and this is my third among the boons"

verses.sanskrit: देवैरत्रापि विचिकित्सितं पुरा न हि सुविज्ञेयमणुरेष धर्मः । अन्यं वरं नचिकेतो वृणीष्व मा मोपरोत्सीरति मा सृजैनम् ॥
verses.translation: (Yama said) : " This subtle matter is not easily understood. In the days of yore, even Gods had entairtained doubts about this. Therefore, O! Nachiketa! choose another boon. Do not press this and release me from this (Obligation)"

verses.sanskrit: देवैरत्रापि विचिकित्सितं किल त्वं च मृत्यो यन्न सुज्ञेयमात्थ । वक्ता चास्य त्वादृगन्यो न लभ्यो नान्यो वरस्तुल्य एतस्य कश्चित् ॥
verses.translation: (Nachiketa said) : " O! Death! you too say that even the gods had indeed doubted about this and is not also easy to comprehend. There is neither a teacher equal to you nor a boon equal to this".

verses.sanskrit: शतायुषः पुत्रपौत्रान्वृणीष्वा बहून्पशून् हस्तिहिरण्यमश्वान् । भूमेर्महदायतनं वृणीष्व स्वयं च जीव शरदो यावदिच्छसि ॥
verses.translation: (Yama said) : "May you seek sons and grandsons who will live a hundred years, cattle, elephants, horses and gold, as also an extensive land area to rule and live any number of autumns as you like.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्तुल्यं यदि मन्यसे वरं वृणीष्व वित्तं चिरजीविकां च । महाभूमौ नचिकेतस्त्वमेधि कामानां त्वा कामभाजं करोमि ॥
verses.translation: If you think so, seek another boon equal to this for riches, longivity and an expansive kingdom where O! Nachiketa ! May you rule over. I shall make you the enjoyer of all your desires.

verses.sanskrit: ये ये कामा दुर्लभा मर्त्यलोके सर्वान् कामाँश्छन्दतः प्रार्थयस्व । इमा रामाः सरथाः सतूर्या न हीदृशा लम्भनीया मनुष्यैः । आभिर्मत्प्रत्ताभिः परिचारयस्व नचिकेतो मरणं माऽनुप्राक्षीः ॥
verses.translation: Whatever desires are there which are not easy to be gained in this world of mortals, all those may you obtain as you desire. These beautiful damsels along with chariots and musical instruments, which are not attainable by men, I shall bestow upon you. Entertain yourself with these. Do not seek to know about death.

verses.sanskrit: श्वोभावा मर्त्यस्य यदन्तकैतत् सर्वेंद्रियाणां जरयन्ति तेजः । अपि सर्वं जीवितमल्पमेव तवैव वाहास्तव नृत्यगीते ॥
verses.translation: "O! Death! All these are impermanent. The sense organs of man is worn out through indulgence in these enjoyments. The entire life is but brief. You may keep these vehicles, music and dance to yourself.

verses.sanskrit: न वित्तेन तर्पणीयो मनुष्यो लप्स्यामहे वित्तमद्राक्ष्म चेत्त्वा । जीविष्यामो यावदीशिष्यसि त्वं वरस्तु मे वरणीयः स एव ॥
verses.translation: No man is ever satisfied with wealth. Having seen you, we shall live as long as you rule. The boon I desire is , therefore , the one I have sought.

verses.sanskrit: अजीर्यताममृतानामुपेत्य जीर्यन्मर्त्यः क्वधःस्थः प्रजानन् । अभिध्यायन् वर्णरतिप्रमोदान् अतिदीर्घे जीविते को रमेत ॥
verses.translation: Once the immortal and everlasting one is attained, which man, who is himself nor permanent, will be elated or be thought of living long after examining the worthless enjoyments of song & dance ?

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन्निदं विचिकित्सन्ति मृत्यो यत्साम्पराये महति ब्रूहि नस्तत् । योऽयं वरो गूढमनुप्रविष्टो नान्यं तस्मान्नचिकेता वृणीते ॥
verses.translation: O! Death! Instruct me on the superior life after death regarding which people have reservations. Nachiketa chooses not other boon other than this mysterious one.
